question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getPendingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user determine the relationship between two specific accounts (sender and receiver) by querying `/api/v1/accounts/{receiverId}/airdrops/pending?sender.id={senderId}` without either account's approval, leaking private financial relationships between accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: processRequest()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Bound` class's `adjustUpperBound()` method — which returns `Long.MAX_VALUE` when no upper bound is set — to implicitly query all records up to the maximum possible ID without the account owner's approval, since the controller does not restrict the effective query range?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `EXTRACTOR` lambda — which extracts `receiverId`, `senderId`, `serialNumber`, and `tokenId` from each airdrop — to obtain a complete financial fingerprint of a victim account's outstanding airdrops without the victim's approval, since the extractor is applied to all response items without access control?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getOutstandingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `DEFAULT_SERIAL_NUMBER = '0L'` constant used in the `EXTRACTOR` lambda to craft follow-up requests using the `next` link that effectively query fungible token airdrops with a serial number of 0, bypassing the intended NFT-only serial number filtering without the account owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/TokenAirdropsController.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getPendingAirdrops()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any CSRF protection or origin validation on the `@GetMapping(value = '/pending')` endpoint to perform cross-site request forgery attacks that leak a victim's pending airdrop data to an attacker-controlled server, without the victim's approval?
